title: hierarchy, containment-acyclic enforcement
manager: Systems nest — a system can contain sub-systems — with the containment tree compile-enforced acyclic even though the wiring between machines may cycle. This separation is the key insight: information can flow in loops, but ownership can't, which is what makes supervision escalation and recursive snapshots well-defined. Containment is a tree; wiring is a graph; keeping them distinct is what keeps the system reasoning sound.
cs: nested systems; containment tree compile-enforced acyclic (wiring may cycle); child dispatch to quiescence, emissions bubble; the ownership structure supervision escalates up.
